color_profiles.name: lavender-lepidolite
color_profiles.cause: Mn2+ and/or Mn3+ substituting in octahedral sites; trace amounts produce pastel lavender.

color_profiles.name: pink-lepidolite
color_profiles.cause: Higher Mn content shifting from lavender toward pink.

color_profiles.name: purple-lepidolite
color_profiles.cause: Elevated Mn3+ content; sometimes enhanced by inclusions.

color_profiles.name: colorless-white-lepidolite
color_profiles.cause: Absence of Mn or other chromophores; high F content typical.

color_profiles.name: yellow-lepidolite
color_profiles.cause: Minor Fe3+ or specific structural defects.

visual_notes: Lepidolite is one of the most visually distinctive micas due to its lavender/pink color — the only common lavender mica. In pegmatites, it often forms spectacular masses of pale lavender scales intergrown with pink tourmaline (rubellite) and rose quartz. The pearly luster on cleavage surfaces gives a soft, shimmering quality.

optical_effects.fluorescence: Weak cream to yellow under SW UV in some specimens; generally weak
optical_effects.iridescence: Subtle adularescence-like pearly shimmer on cleavage surfaces
optical_effects.play_of_color: Subtle adularescence-like pearly shimmer on cleavage surfaces
